summary: Legacy validated record without machine-checkable validation proof. Official Windows service control for the PrintNotify service. Microsoft documents the service name, default start mode, and says it is OK to disable. The app disables the service by setting its start mode to Disabled.

setting.name: Print Notification Service
setting.casual_explanation: This service opens custom printer dialogs and handles printer notifications. Disabling it removes those printer notifications and related extension dialogs.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it on systems that rarely print or on minimal builds where printer pop-ups and extensions are not needed.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling it removes printer notifications and custom printer dialog extensions, but it can simplify systems that never use those features.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft's Windows IoT Enterprise service guidance lists Print Notification Service `(PrintNotify)` with a default start mode of `Manual` and marks it `OK to disable`. Microsoft also notes that turning the service off means printer extensions and notifications will not be shown. The app uses a service start-mode tweak that disables `PrintNotify`.

allowed_values.label: Windows default
allowed_values.meaning: Print Notification Service starts on demand with Microsoft's documented default mode.

allowed_values.label: Printer notifications disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Windows no longer starts PrintNotify until its start mode is restored.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app disables PrintNotify by setting its start mode to Disabled.

windows_defaults.label: Microsoft documented default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems using the normal PrintNotify configuration

states.rationale: Microsoft lists PrintNotify with a Manual default start mode.

windows_defaults.notes: This baseline preserves printer notifications and extension dialogs.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems that use printers and printer notifications
avoid_for: Minimal systems that intentionally do not want printer notifications

states.rationale: Keep the documented default service mode.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want printer notifications and custom printer dialogs available.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You never use those printing features.
tradeoffs: Printer notifications and extensions remain available.
The service can still start when Windows needs it.

recommended_profiles.label: No printer notifications
intended_for: Minimal builds without printer pop-up needs
avoid_for: Users who expect printer extensions or remote printer notifications
recommended_profiles.good_when: You intentionally do not want printer notifications or extension dialogs.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need printer notifications or custom printer dialogs.
tradeoffs: Printer extensions and notifications are no longer shown.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Guidance on configuring system services - Print Notification Service
evidence.summary: Microsoft lists Print Notification Service `(PrintNotify)` with start mode `Manual` and marks it `OK to disable`, noting that printer extensions and notifications will not be shown if it is turned off.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider disables the `PrintNotify` service through the batch service start-mode tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the PrintNotify service name, its Manual default start mode, the printer-notification behavior it provides, and that it is OK to disable in the service guidance used here. The app's service-start-mode action matches that documented control surface cleanly.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Printer Extensions and Notifications (PrintNotify); Manual; If this service is turned off, printer extensions and notifications are not shown.; OK to disable: This service provides functionality that is useful to some but not all enterprises, and security-focused enterprises that don't use it can safely disable it.
validation_proof.notes: The Microsoft services guidance page lists the PrintNotify service under Printer Extensions and Notifications, gives its Manual baseline, describes the effect of turning it off, and marks it OK to disable.
